discussion_comments.comment_body:
I don't think we need to load plugins at all.

There are two major concepts: Components (x-component) and Extension points (x-extension)

Components:
If we have <x-component path="plugins/components/test/hello.html">, it will be handled automatically by the addition in run_ui.py for fetching from plugins prefix.
 The backend will search all plugins roots for the existing plugin/file and return it.
We do not use /usr/plugins or /usr/project/plugins here, the backend will find out where the plugin is.
This way we can import components as well as javascript files from any plugin just like we do now the globals.

Extensions:
<x-extension id="location_a"> is an extension point.
When we find extension point in the HTML, we should call backend get_webui_extensions(id) and backend will search all plugin directories with subdir /extensions/webui/{id}/*.html, send us array of files and contents and it will paste them into the <x-extension>HERE</x-extension>

I think we forgot to add this in the default implementation in setMessages(). 
I will modify dev branch to return more unified format from message handlers, the log number will be there.
